verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (cysteine-rich secretory protein 1) is a secreted glycoprotein with a single-pass transmembrane domain. It is a member of the CAP superfamily and is expressed in reproductive tissues, particularly in the epididymis and seminal plasma. The protein is membrane-anchored via its TM domain and has documented surface display on sperm and epithelial cells. It functions in reproductive biology and immune modulation. The TM domain provides stable membrane anchoring on the outer leaflet; the bulk of the protein extends extracellularly. It qualifies as a bona-fide surface-accessible protein with its own membrane anchor.
